# Explore 5x setup (four voters, one witness). The voters should be thought
# of as being spread over two availability zones.
setup
v1: [a1]
v2: [a1]
v3: [a1]
v4: [a1]
w
----
v1: [a1] term=a
v2: [a1] term=a
v3: [a1] term=a
v4: [a1] term=a

# Initially, all is well and v1 wins leadership. The witness votes too, though
# not strictly required.
campaign v1
----
v1 campaigns at term b
v1: yes (self)
v2:
  - term advance a -> b
  - granted vote for v1 at term b
  yes
v3:
  - term advance a -> b
  - granted vote for v1 at term b
  yes
v4:
  - term advance a -> b
  - granted vote for v1 at term b
  yes
w:
  - granted vote for v1 at term b, hi carried as 0
  yes
outcome: won

# v4 goes dark.
set-avail down=(v4)
----
ok

# v1 proposes an entry and appends. v4 (which is down) is not required for
# quorum.
propose v1
----
v1: [a1 b2 b3] term=b leader
v2: [a1] term=b
v3: [a1] term=b
v4: [a1] term=b

append v1
----
v1 appends through b3
v2:
  - accepted append through b3 (ok)
  ok
v3:
  - accepted append through b3 (ok)
  ok
v4: (dropped)
v1: [a1 b2 b3] term=b committed=b3 leader
v2: [a1 b2 b3] term=b
v3: [a1 b2 b3] term=b
v4: [a1] term=b

# v1 proposes a second entry.
propose v1
----
v1: [a1 b2 b3 b4] term=b committed=b3 leader
v2: [a1 b2 b3] term=b
v3: [a1 b2 b3] term=b
v4: [a1] term=b

# Now v3 is down too (AZ outage, for example).
set-avail down=(v3)
----
ok

# Quorum can't be reached, only v2 is around to ack.
append v1
----
v1 appends through b4
v2:
  - accepted append through b4 (ok)
  ok
v3: (dropped)
v4: (dropped)
v1: [a1 b2 b3 b4] term=b committed=b3 leader
v2: [a1 b2 b3 b4] term=b
v3: [a1 b2 b3] term=b
v4: [a1] term=b

# Realizing this, the leader can engage the witness.
engage v1
----
w:
  - engaged at b4
  ok
w:  vote=(b, v1) acked=(b4, engaged)
v1: [a1 b2 b3 b4] term=b committed=b4 leader

# If v2 were to campaign, v1 would vote for it (log is up to date),
# but the witness wouldn't. Since v3 and v4 are down, v2 can't win
# an election at this point - the witness would need to be released
# first.
prevote v2
----
v2 prevotes at term c
v2: yes (self)
v1:
  - term advance b -> c
  - stepped down as leader
  - granted vote for v2 at term c
  yes (prevote, no state change)
v3: (dropped)
v4: (dropped)
w:
  - engaged: effectiveHi inflated to b∞
  - rejected vote for v2: log not up to date (effectiveHi b∞ > lm b4)
  no
outcome: lost

# Another entry on v1, now v2 is not up to date with it any longer.
propose v1
----
v1: [a1 b2 b3 b4 b5] term=b committed=b4 leader
v2: [a1 b2 b3 b4] term=b
v3: [a1 b2 b3] term=b
v4: [a1] term=b

# Correspondingly, v2's pre-vote now gets rejected even by v1.
prevote v2
----
v2 prevotes at term c
v2: yes (self)
v1:
  - term advance b -> c
  - stepped down as leader
  - rejected vote for v2: log not up to date (b5 > b4)
  no, log not up to date (b5 > b4)
v3: (dropped)
v4: (dropped)
w:
  - engaged: effectiveHi inflated to b∞
  - rejected vote for v2: log not up to date (effectiveHi b∞ > lm b4)
  no
outcome: lost

# v1 goes down. v3 comes up in its stead, but this is not enough.
set-avail down=(v1) up=(v3)
----
ok

# The best candidate is v2, but even it can't win because the witness
# is still engaged at the previous term.
prevote v2
----
v2 prevotes at term c
v2: yes (self)
v1: (dropped)
v3:
  - term advance b -> c
  - granted vote for v2 at term c
  yes (prevote, no state change)
v4: (dropped)
w:
  - engaged: effectiveHi inflated to b∞
  - rejected vote for v2: log not up to date (effectiveHi b∞ > lm b4)
  no
outcome: lost

# We need a traditional quorum to forcibly release the witness.
set-avail up=(v4)
----
ok

# v2 can win, though the witness rejected the vote (as it should) and remains
# engaged at the old term.
campaign v2
----
v2 campaigns at term c
v2: yes (self)
v1: (dropped)
v3:
  - term advance b -> c
  - granted vote for v2 at term c
  yes
v4:
  - term advance b -> c
  - granted vote for v2 at term c
  yes
w:
  - engaged: effectiveHi inflated to b∞
  - rejected vote for v2: log not up to date (effectiveHi b∞ > lm b4)
  no
outcome: won

# v2 could release the witness (engaging it would also work), but instead we
# demonstrate that this is not necessary in future steps. That is, in production
# we likely don't need code that proactively makes sure that the witness is
# released following a successful campaign.
#
# v2 just won a traditional-quorum election, so sensibly it tries to append to
# the followers that voted for it.
# In doing so, everybody now has a log entry whose LogTerm is newer than the
# witness's engaged term. This means they can get votes from the witness
# and break the engagement in the process.
append v2
----
v2 appends through c5
v1: (dropped)
v3:
  - accepted append through c5 (ok)
  ok
v4:
  - accepted append through c5 (ok)
  ok
v1: [a1 b2 b3 b4 b5] term=b committed=b4 leader
v2: [a1 b2 b3 b4 c5] term=c leader
v3: [a1 b2 b3 b4 c5] term=c
v4: [a1 b2 b3 b4 c5] term=c

# Pre-vote succeeds, note that the witness would vote and release the engagement.
prevote v4
----
v4 prevotes at term d
v4: yes (self)
v1: (dropped)
v2:
  - term advance c -> d
  - stepped down as leader
  - granted vote for v4 at term d
  yes (prevote, no state change)
v3:
  - term advance c -> d
  - granted vote for v4 at term d
  yes (prevote, no state change)
w:
  - engaged: effectiveHi inflated to b∞
  - granted vote for v4 at term d, hi carried as b∞
  yes (prevote, no state change)
outcome: won
